_meta.purpose: Per-deal shareholder background research for the Upcoming-IPO section: flags conglomerate ties, PEP (Politically Exposed Person) status, listed-company affiliations and foreign-strategic holders.
_meta.method: Web research per named shareholder (Indonesian financial press, IDX/e-IPO disclosures, company sites, prospectus). Namesake-cautious: shared surnames (e.g. Widjaja, Hartono, Supra) are NOT treated as links without direct evidence. BACH is derived from its prospectus forensic; the other five from dated web sources cited inline.
_meta.disclaimer: Not financial advice. Research compiled from public sources and may contain namesake or attribution errors. Items marked confidence 'low' or 'unverified', and any dated/unproven reputational mentions, are explicitly uncertain and should be confirmed against the final prospectus before relying on them.

tagLegend.conglomerate: Controlled by or part of a large business group
tagLegend.pep: Politically Exposed Person — holds/held government, party or state-enterprise office
tagLegend.affiliated-listed: Holding by, or cross-holding with, an IDX-listed company
tagLegend.unverified: Identity or affiliation not reliably established

shareholders.name: PT Bach Multi Sukses Investama (BMSI)
shareholders.finding: Founding Bach-family holding company and the pre-option controller (52.28% post-IPO before the option). It is selling command of the company: it sells 1,042,227,300 shares to GTP via a negotiated-market crossing within five working days of listing, dropping to 26.77%. A control sale, not a clean exit.

shareholders.name: PT Global Telekomunikasi Prima (GTP)
shareholders.finding: Incoming controller and the Hartono-group acquisition vehicle. Holds 25.48% at listing and exercises a pre-agreed irrevocable option (signed 7 Jan 2026, exercise notice 13 Mar 2026) to reach 51% within five working days of listing. The prospectus designates GTP 'controller' by resolution on 8 Jan 2026 while it still held only 30%, the basis for asserting no mandatory tender offer is triggered.

shareholders.name: Martin Basuki Hartono & Victor Rachmat Hartono (UBOs via GTP)
shareholders.finding: Named in the prospectus as the ultimate beneficial owners of incoming controller GTP. They are the Hartono family of the Djarum / BCA / GDP Venture group, which also controls IDX-listed tower group Sarana Menara Nusantara (TOWR) and Protelindo, BACH's largest customer.

shareholders.name: Budi Kurniawan, Hartanto Rahardja, Edy Surianto, Zulfahmi Fithri, Hasby Jap
shareholders.finding: The five Bach founders/management (Dir. Utama, Komisaris, directors), each holding ~1.3-1.7% post-IPO. They retain operating roles for management continuity as the Hartono group takes economic control; they are unlocked (no eight-month lock-up asserted) and have no ESA/ESOP.

dealExposure.summary: BACH wraps a genset/telecom-infra business around a control hand-over from the founding Bach family (BMSI) to the Hartono family (Djarum / BCA / GDP Venture) via GTP, which steps from 25.5% at listing to 51% through a pre-agreed post-listing option structured to avoid a mandatory tender offer. The Hartono UBOs also control listed tower group TOWR and Protelindo, which is simultaneously BACH's largest customer (~14-40% of revenue), so a large slice of revenue becomes related-party post-listing.
flags: Conglomerate control transfer to the Hartono/Djarum group via a GTP option (25.5% to 51%), structured to assert no mandatory tender offer for minorities
Chair (Komut Anita Anwar) is concurrently VP Director of Protelindo, Director of TOWR and Komut of listed REMALA, sitting on both sides of BACH's largest customer relationship
~14-40% of revenue becomes related-party (Protelindo / TOWR group) after the acquisition
Only 1 of 3 commissioners is independent (33%, bare minimum); float sits at the 15.06% regulatory floor

shareholders.name: Raffi Farid Ahmad
shareholders.finding: Sole controller and ultimate beneficial owner; celebrity entertainer and co-founder of RANS. A Politically Exposed Person: sworn in 22 October 2024 before President Prabowo Subianto as Utusan Khusus Presiden (Presidential Special Envoy) for Development of the Young Generation and Art Workers (2024-2029), under Keppres No. 76/M of 2024.

shareholders.name: PT Indonesia Entertainmen Grup
shareholders.finding: Content sub-holding of PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (IDX: SCMA), part of the Emtek Group (IDX: EMTK), owner of SCTV and Indosiar. The Emtek conglomerate's strategic stake in RANS (originally part of a ~Rp248bn / up to 17% investment). Its Direktur Utama is Emtek/SCMA chief Sutanto Hartono.

shareholders.name: Soultan Ariq Rachman
shareholders.finding: Investment professional at Provident Capital Partners (Jakarta); appears to hold as a private financial investor, not in any government capacity. No PEP status found. The Provident/Saratoga network also links to Bali United, consistent with another investor entity on this cap table.

shareholders.name: Dony Oskaria
shareholders.finding: Senior state-enterprise official and a clear PEP: appointed Wakil Menteri BUMN (inaugurated 20 Oct 2024), COO of Danantara (24 Feb 2025), acting Minister of BUMN (from 17 Sep 2025) and Kepala BP BUMN (from 8 Oct 2025, Keppres No. 109/P of 2025).

shareholders.name: Sutanto Hartono
shareholders.finding: Senior Emtek media executive: Managing Director of Emtek and Direktur Utama of PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (SCMA), Vidio, SCTV and PT Indonesia Entertainmen Grup. Stake reflects the Emtek/SCMA affiliation. NAMESAKE NOTE: NOT a member of the Djarum/BCA Hartono family; 'Hartono' is a common Indonesian name and his ties are to Emtek/SCMA only.

shareholders.name: Nagita Slavina Mariana Tengker
shareholders.finding: Co-founder of RANS and spouse of controller/UBO Raffi Ahmad; PEP-family by virtue of that marriage. No independent government or political office of her own was found.

shareholders.name: Kaesang Pangarep
shareholders.finding: PEP on two counts: youngest son of former President Joko Widodo (PEP-family), and General Chairman (Ketua Umum) of the PSI party, re-elected for 2025-2030 at the September 2025 PSI Congress.

shareholders.name: Hikmat Janika
shareholders.finding: Minority individual shareholder; identity not reliably established. One outlet (suara.com) identifies him as a public-hospital facilities official (Kepala IPSR, RSUD Pasar Minggu), but this single-thread description is unexplained relative to RANS and may be a namesake mismatch. No conglomerate, listed-company or political tie confirmed.

shareholders.name: PT Ekonomi Baru Investasi Teknologi
shareholders.finding: Investment subsidiary (~90% owned) of PT Bali Bintang Sejahtera Tbk (IDX: BOLA), the listed operator of the Bali United football club, set up Sep 2021 to invest in new-economy technology. The stake ties to the Bali United / Provident-Saratoga investor sphere.

dealExposure.summary: RANS is a founder-controlled media holding (Raffi Ahmad 62.93% post-IPO, with wife Nagita Slavina) carrying unusually dense political exposure: the controlling owner is himself a sitting Presidential Special Envoy, while the register also includes SOE regulatory chief Dony Oskaria and PSI chairman Kaesang Pangarep (son of ex-President Jokowi). It is simultaneously conglomerate-linked through Emtek/SCMA (PT Indonesia Entertainmen Grup ~7.2% plus Emtek CEO Sutanto Hartono) and the listed Bali United group. A celebrity-and-politically-connected float where governance, related-party and headline risk weigh heavily.
flags: Controlling UBO Raffi Ahmad is a sitting government official (Presidential Special Envoy)
Multiple PEPs on the register: Dony Oskaria (Head of BP BUMN / acting Minister of BUMN / Danantara COO) and Kaesang Pangarep (PSI chairman, son of former President Jokowi)
Emtek/SCMA conglomerate affiliation via PT Indonesia Entertainmen Grup and CEO Sutanto Hartono, implying related-party content/distribution dependence
Float is small (~20%) with the founder retaining majority control
Hikmat Janika beneficial identity unverified; confirm against the prospectus

shareholders.name: Surya Gunawan Widjaja
shareholders.finding: Founder, controlling shareholder (PSP) and Komisaris Utama; age 72. Self-made medical-device veteran (~36 years) who founded EMMI in 2000 and built the family-owned 'Esa Group' of device makers (ESA Tech in Solo, Surgitec in Cikarang). No conglomerate, PEP, or other listed-company ties; an industry entrepreneur, not a public figure.

shareholders.name: Andrew Ignatius Widjaja
shareholders.finding: Controlling-family member and a Komisaris of EMMI (23% pre-IPO); founding family's next generation working inside the company. No conglomerate, PEP, or other listed-firm role. Unrelated to the Sinar Mas Widjaja family (shared surname only).

shareholders.name: Florian Chris Widjaja
shareholders.finding: Controlling-family member and Direktur Utama (CEO) of EMMI; age ~40 (16% pre-IPO). Profiled as the young operating leader of the family business. No conglomerate/PEP ties; not connected to the Sinar Mas Widjaja family.

shareholders.name: Andrian Matthew Widjaja
shareholders.finding: Controlling-family member and a Direktur of EMMI (16% pre-IPO). Public footprint is that of a private individual in the family device business. No conglomerate/PEP ties; unrelated to the Sinar Mas Widjaja family.

shareholders.name: Eddy Lie
shareholders.finding: Long-standing co-founder/business partner (35% at the 2000 founding, 15% pre-IPO) and a Direktur of EMMI; age 64. Represents a second founding family (a 'Jason Aaron Lie' also sits on the board). No conglomerate, PEP, or reputational/legal issues found.

dealExposure.summary: EMMI is a closely held, family-controlled medical-device distribution and light-manufacturing business. The Widjaja family retains ~59% post-IPO, led by 72-year-old founder Surya Gunawan Widjaja, with the next generation (Florian as CEO, Andrew and Andrian on the board) running operations alongside co-founder Eddy Lie. A self-made operating family, not a conglomerate vehicle. No PEPs, no cross-holdings in other listed companies, and no reputational or legal red flags surfaced in news or court-record searches.
flags: Surname 'Widjaja' coincides with the Sinar Mas founding family but there is NO evidence of any link; do not infer a conglomerate connection from the name
Concentrated family control (~59% post-IPO) with only ~30% genuine public float, standard minority-shareholder governance considerations for a founder-controlled small-cap
Younger Widjajas' exact familial link to the founder is implied by surname/roles rather than stated in the sources reviewed (medium confidence)

shareholders.name: PT Sarana Meditama Metropolitan Tbk (SAME)
shareholders.finding: IDX-listed hospital operator behind EMC Hospitals (formerly OMNI), ~75% controlled by PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi Tbk (EMTK/Emtek), the media-tech-healthcare group of Eddy Kusnadi Sariaatmadja. SAME first bought 28% of Nitrasanata Dharma in April 2022; post-IPO it is the single largest holder at 25.2% but explicitly NON-controlling. Makes JECX an Emtek-associated cross-holding by a listed peer.

shareholders.name: Dr. Darwan Madja Purba, Sp.M(K)
shareholders.finding: One of the four ophthalmologists who founded Jakarta Eye Center (then Klinik Mata Jakarta) on 1 Feb 1984. Designated the UBO of JECX: beyond his 11.8% direct stake he is the single largest owner (19.78%) of the controlling vehicle Magna Selaras Lestari. A senior JEC eye surgeon, not a businessman-controller; no conglomerate or political ties.

shareholders.name: PT Magna Selaras Lestari (MSL)
shareholders.finding: The controlling shareholder (committed to retain control 12 months post-IPO). A doctor-and-employee holding vehicle, not a conglomerate: owners include Dr. Darwan Purba (19.78%), Dr. Waldensius Girsang (10.48%), Drg. Rifi Arianti (6.97%), Prof. Widya Artini Wiyogo (6.83%) and the JEC Employee Cooperative (3.92%). Control sits collectively with JEC's founding doctors and staff.

shareholders.name: Rayyan Farrel Wardhana Istiantoro
shareholders.finding: Individual shareholder. The 'Istiantoro' surname matches the late Prof. Istiantoro, one of JEC's four 1984 co-founders, so he is almost certainly a founding-family descendant holding inherited equity (shares the 'Wardhana Istiantoro' name with Nabbil, likely a sibling). The specific lineage is inferred from the surname and JEC's founder roster, not an explicit prospectus statement. No PEP/conglomerate/adverse findings.

shareholders.name: Nabbil Wardhana Istiantoro
shareholders.finding: Individual shareholder sharing the 'Wardhana Istiantoro' name with Rayyan Farrel, indicating siblings and almost certainly descendants of JEC co-founder the late Prof. Istiantoro. Founding-family link inferred from surname/founder history rather than explicit disclosure. No PEP status, conglomerate tie or reputational issue found.

shareholders.name: DR. Dr. Waldensius Girsang, Sp.M(K)
shareholders.finding: Selling shareholder divesting ~162.66m shares (5% of capital) in the IPO. A senior JEC vitreoretinal/cataract ophthalmologist who is also the second-largest owner (10.48%) of controller Magna Selaras Lestari. One dated reputational item: a 2007 police report by a patient alleged blindness after laser treatment and named him and Dr. Darwan Purba, an unproven ~19-year-old allegation with no documented finding of wrongdoing. No political or conglomerate ties.

shareholders.name: Prof. DR. Dr. Widya Artini Wiyogo, Sp.M
shareholders.finding: Senior JEC glaucoma/cataract ophthalmologist and Professor in Ophthalmology at FK UI (Head of Glaucoma Division since 2006). Holds 6.83% of controller Magna Selaras Lestari; her direct JECX stake was not disclosed in the sources reviewed. Academic/medical affiliation only; no PEP, conglomerate, or adverse findings.

dealExposure.summary: JECX is the operator of Jakarta Eye Center (JEC), Indonesia's leading eye-hospital group founded in 1984; the ticker and 'JEC' brand are the same institution. Control rests with the founding doctors and employees via PT Magna Selaras Lestari (largest UBO Dr. Darwan Purba), a medical-professional partnership rather than a tycoon. The headline flag is the 25.2% NON-controlling stake held by listed peer SAME (EMC/OMNI Hospitals), ~75% owned by Emtek (EMTK) of the Sariaatmadja group, making JECX conglomerate-linked at the strategic-shareholder level. No PEPs identified.
flags: Conglomerate-linked: 25.2% held by listed SAME, ~75% controlled by Emtek/EMTK (Sariaatmadja family)
Controller is a doctor/employee holding vehicle (Magna Selaras Lestari), concentrating founding-family/insider influence
Two ~8.9% Istiantoro holders are inferred (not prospectus-confirmed) to be founding-family heirs of the late co-founder Prof. Istiantoro
Minor/dated reputational item: a 2007 unproven malpractice police report named JEC doctors; no documented finding of wrongdoing
Selling shareholder Dr. Waldensius Girsang is cashing out the entire 5% secondary tranche at IPO

shareholders.name: PT Niramas Utama International (NUI)
shareholders.finding: Controlling shareholder (PSP) and intermediate holding company within the Supra Group, a family conglomerate controlled by Yusuf Hamdani. Ownership chain: Yusuf controls ~93.79% of PT Supra Sentral Investama > ~89.9% of PT Supra Pangan Lestari > ~89.9% of NUI > 99.8% of JELI pre-IPO. Supra Group also spans PT Supra Sekuritas (IDX-member broker), BPR Supra Artapersada (rural bank) and property (Pancoran Chinatown/Glodok).

shareholders.name: Yusuf Hamdani (Ham Pak Japyusuf Hamdani) — UBO
shareholders.finding: Ultimate beneficial owner and senior conglomerate figure (age ~85). Dirut of PT Niramas Utama since 2013 and of NUI since 2002; founder/controller of Supra Group across manufacturing, distribution, financial services and plantations. Family-run: Philip Hamdani is a JELI director; Sonny Hamdani also holds Supra Sekuritas shares. JELI is the group's first IPO. No political/government role and no legal/reputational issues surfaced.

shareholders.name: Sadikun Wiratno
shareholders.finding: Komisaris Utama with a token 0.15% stake; not part of the Hamdani controlling family. No roles at other IDX-listed companies, no conglomerate control, no political/government position found. An ICIJ Offshore Leaks record names a 'Sadikun Wiratno' (Jakarta) as a 2004-era shareholder of Lighthouse Holdings Pte Ltd (Singapore), a possible but UNCONFIRMED namesake; identity not verified and offshore holding alone is not evidence of wrongdoing.

dealExposure.summary: JELI (the Inaco nata-de-coco/jelly maker) is family-controlled through a multi-layer holding chain (~74% post-IPO) by Yusuf Hamdani's Supra Group, a senior but low-profile Indonesian conglomerate spanning F&B, a securities broker (Supra Sekuritas), a rural bank and property. This is the group's first listing, so there is no public-market track record. No PEP ties, no other listed-company affiliations, and no legal/reputational red flags found for the controller or the president commissioner.
flags: Concentrated family control (~74%) via a 3-layer holding structure; minority public float ~26%
First-time issuer from the Supra Group, no prior listed-company governance track record
Founder/UBO is ~85 years old, succession/key-person risk
Group is related to a securities broker (Supra Sekuritas) and a BPR, watch for related-party transactions
Unconfirmed namesake: an ICIJ Offshore Leaks 'Sadikun Wiratno' (Singapore entity, 2004) may or may not be the JELI commissioner
IPO proceeds include ~10.6% to repay a related short-term bank loan rather than fully funding growth

shareholders.name: PT Prodia Utama
shareholders.finding: Controlling shareholder and the family holding company of the Prodia group, owned in roughly equal ~16.67% shares by the six Prodia founders (Andi Widjaja, Gunawan Prawiro Soeharto, Hanoko Suryadi, Elisabeth Listijani Widjojo, Justini Hidajat, Arjati Utami). Also the controlling shareholder of listed parent PRDA (57%). No conglomerate or government ties; an independent founder-family vehicle.

shareholders.name: Six Prodia founders (UBOs of PT Prodia Utama)
shareholders.finding: Andi Widjaja, Gunawan Prawiro Soeharto, Hanoko Suryadi, Elisabeth Listijani Widjojo, Justini Hidajat and Arjati Utami, each ~16.67% of Prodia Utama. Lead founder Andi Wijaya (b. 1936) is a documented scientist-entrepreneur (ITB pharmacy 1963, PhD molecular biology Munster 1978, academic at Universitas Hasanuddin); his only formal positions found are academic, not governmental. No PEP status, political office, or conglomerate affiliation found; the original lab was founded in Solo in 1973.

shareholders.name: PT Prodia Widyahusada Tbk (PRDA)
shareholders.finding: The IDX-listed Prodia clinical-laboratory chain (PRDA) and operating parent of PRDL; affiliated-listed. PRDA is itself controlled by PT Prodia Utama (~57%). Note: media reported PT Chandra Asri Pacific (TPIA, Prajogo Pangestu / Barito) quietly bought ~1.48% of PRDA in April-May 2026 (plus ~4.6% via Henan Putihrai-managed funds), but these are small minority stakes one level up, not control, and TPIA holds no direct stake in PRDL.

shareholders.name: Diasys Diagnostic Systems GmbH
shareholders.finding: German in-vitro diagnostics company (Holzheim, est. 1991), maker of liquid-stable clinical-chemistry reagents/analyzers, fitting PRDL's IVD focus. Material note: since 1 December 2023 DiaSys is 75% owned by Shenzhen Mindray Bio-Medical (China, acquired ~EUR 115m), so this 7% stake is effectively held by a Mindray-majority-controlled subsidiary rather than a fully independent German firm.

dealExposure.summary: PRDL is a clean, founder-family-controlled deal: the IVD-manufacturing arm of the Prodia laboratory group, controlled by PT Prodia Utama (35.7%) owned equally by six independent Prodia founders, alongside listed parent PRDA (27.3%) and a 7% foreign strategic stake from Germany's DiaSys. No PEPs, no Indonesian conglomerate in control. Two secondary notes worth knowing rather than fearing: the DiaSys stake is effectively held by China's Mindray (75% owner since Dec 2023), and Prajogo Pangestu's Barito group holds a small ~1.48% minority in the listed parent PRDA (not in PRDL itself).
flags: Foreign strategic shareholder DiaSys is 75% controlled by China's Shenzhen Mindray since Dec 2023, so the 7% is effectively a Mindray-affiliated stake

Prajogo Pangestu / Barito group (TPIA) holds a small ~1.48% minority in listed parent PRDA (plus ~4.6% via Henan Putihrai funds) acquired just before the IPO; non-controlling and one level removed from PRDL, but flagged by media as quiet accumulation
